seoTitle: Southwest vs Sprent — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Southwest and Sprent. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Southwest and Sprent.
heading: Southwest vs Sprent — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Southwest, Tasmania and Sprent, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sprent has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Southwest (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Southwest or Sprent safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Southwest has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Sprent scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Southwest scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Sprent scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Southwest or Sprent?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sprent has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.23% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
